outcome_contract.why: Staff identity is caller-controlled at the Prime API layer â€” any holder of the gateway token can attribute actions to any staff UID, including audit records, broadcast sends, and message attributions. This is a correctness and audit-integrity issue, and for broadcast specifically it is a direct-harm risk (messages to all active guests attributed to wrong person).
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: After this change, the actor UID recorded at the Prime layer is cryptographically bound to a short-lived signed claims header verified with a separate signing secret. A caller with only the gateway token cannot forge a different UID. The broadcast send path additionally requires the caller to hold the `owner` or `admin` role (exact allowlist, not hierarchical â€” roles are a flat set).

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No UI changes â€” server-side only
Shared token validates caller but does not bind identity; actor UID is freely injectable; broadcast role gate absent
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: **High gap**: C-04 UID forgery + L-04 unbounded broadcast
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes â€” core of this work

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Forged UIDs corrupt audit trail; post-fix old records not retroactively corrected

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: HMAC verification is O(1) CPU; adds one WebCrypto call per request; negligible on CF Workers

stage_payload.scope_summary: The Prime messaging staff API endpoints use a shared static gateway token (`PRIME_STAFF_OWNER_GATE_TOKEN`) or Cloudflare Access session cookie to authenticate callers, but then trust the caller-supplied `x-prime-actor-uid` request header to identify which staff member is acting. Any caller who holds the gateway token can assert any UID without server-side verification. This applies to **8** staff-facing mutation endpoints: `staff-initiate-thread`, `review-thread-draft`, `review-thread-resolve`, `review-thread-dismiss`, `review-thread-send`, `review-campaign-send`, `review-campaign` (POST creat
non_goals: Rebuilding the full multi-staff auth system for Prime (out of scope; currently a single shared PIN).
Adding per-request Firebase token verification on all Prime endpoints (the CF Worker environment lacks the Firebase Admin SDK).
constraints: Prime runs as Cloudflare Pages Functions; no Firebase Admin SDK, no Node.js crypto beyond WebCrypto.
assumptions: The correct minimal fix requires a **separate** signing secret (`PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET`) present in both the reception app env and the Prime Pages Functions env, independent of `PRIME_STAFF_OWNER_GATE_TOKEN`. Reception signs a short-lived claims payload; Prime verifies. A forger who holds only the gateway token cannot mint valid claims without the separate signing secret.
`requireStaffAuth` in reception already validates the Firebase ID token and extracts UID and roles â€” these are the authoritative values to sign.
Broadcast role gate requires `owner` or `admin`. `developer` is excluded (see Resolved questions).
key_entry_points: `apps/prime/functions/api/staff-initiate-thread.ts` â€” creates broadcast thread + initial draft; reads actor UID from header (line 43)
`apps/reception/src/app/api/mcp/inbox/prime-compose/route.ts` â€” reception route that drives the full broadcast initiation; verified auth at line 13; forwards verified uid at lines 42â€“44
`apps/reception/src/app/api/mcp/inbox/[threadId]/send/route.ts` â€” send-existing-thread path; verified auth, forwards uid at lines 45â€“69

rehearsal_issues.Scope Area: Actor UID consumption on all 8 mutation endpoints
rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: Pattern confirmed across all 8 (6 initial + 2 in review-campaign POST/PUT via critique); no outliers
Correctly implements Firebase ID token verification + role lookup
`staff-initiate-thread` and `review-campaign-send` identified; rate-limit bypass risk noted
Tests cover access gate; zero tests for UID handling or role gate
Firebase RTDB `userProfiles/{uid}` confirmed; roles structure documented
Backward-compat period needed; no feature flag infrastructure today

process_topology.trigger: Staff member in the reception app initiates a whole-hostel broadcast via the "Prime compose" UI.
process_topology.end_condition: Message is queued for delivery to all active guests; draft is created in the Prime D1 database attributed to a staff UID; telemetry event is recorded in the reception database.

areas.label: Reception auth (verified)
areas.flow: Staff fires POST /api/mcp/inbox/prime-compose â†’ `requireStaffAuth` validates Firebase ID token via Identity Toolkit API â†’ retrieves roles from RTDB `userProfiles/{uid}` â†’ returns `{ uid, roles }`
areas.issues: None; this layer is correctly gated

areas.label: Receptionâ†’Prime forwarding
areas.flow: `initiatePrimeOutboundThread({ text, actorUid: auth.uid })` called â†’ adds `x-prime-actor-uid: <uid>` header â†’ calls Prime `POST /api/staff-initiate-thread` with gateway token
areas.issues: UID is forwarded as a plain header â€” no signature

areas.label: Prime gateway enforcement
areas.flow: `enforceStaffOwnerApiGate` checks: if dev, pass; if `PRIME_ENABLE_STAFF_OWNER_ROUTES=true`, pass; else check `cf-access-authenticated-user-email` or `x-prime-access-token == PRIME_STAFF_OWNER_GATE_TOKEN`
areas.issues: Validates caller has token; does NOT bind token to any specific identity

areas.label: Prime actor UID consumption
areas.evidence: 'prime-owner'` â€” trusted as-is; recorded in draft, mutations, and campaign records

areas.label: Broadcast send execution
areas.flow: `review-campaign-send.ts` reads actorUid from header â†’ passes to `sendPrimeReviewCampaign` as actorSource `reception_proxy` â†’ no role check on actorUid
areas.evidence: **L-04: No broadcast role gate** â€” any valid gateway token holder can trigger broadcast
